modified: 2026/06/04
tags:
    - attack.initial_access

    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:

        EventID:

            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection

    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity

items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — Hitachi Energy ITT600 Explorer — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-06-04T15:57:56Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "Hitachi Energy ITT600 Explorer", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX Hitachi Energy ITT600 Explorer Exploitation Attempt - GENERIC"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:9055065; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-06-04, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)

items.title: Starlette has missing Host header validation that poisons request.url.path, bypassing path-based security checks
items.description:
### Summary
In affected versions, the HTTP `Host` request header was not validated before being used to reconstruct `request.url`. Because the routing algorithm relies on the raw HTTP path while `request.url` is rebuilt from the `Host` header, a malformed header could make `request.url.path` differ from the path that was actually requested. Middleware and endpoints that apply security restrictions based on `request.url` (rather than the raw `scope` path) could therefore be bypassed.

### Details
When a client requests `http://example.com/foo`, it sends:

```http
GET /foo HTTP/1.1
Host: example.com
```

Affected versions reconstructed the URL by concatenating `http://{host}{path}` and re-parsing the result. The `Host` value is only valid as a `uri-host [ ":" port ]` per [RFC 9112 §3.2](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9112.html#section-3.2-6), where `uri-host` follows the restricted `host` grammar of [RFC 3986 §3.2.2](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc3986.html#section-3.2.2). When it

items.title: React Router vulnerable to Denial of Service via reflected user input in single-fetch
items.description:
A DoS vulnerability exists in the React Router v7 [Framework Mode](https://reactrouter.com/start/modes#framework), as well as Remix v2.9.0+ with [Single Fetch](https://v2.remix.run/docs/guides/single-fetch) enabled. In some scenarios the underlying serialization algorithm can become a bottleneck when encoding specific types of data into server responses.  Please upgrade to React Router v7.14.0 or later.

> [!NOTE]
> This does not impact your React Router application if you are using [Declarative Mode](https://reactrouter.com/start/modes#declarative) (`<BrowserRouter>`) or [Data Mode](https://reactrouter.com/start/modes#data) (`createBrowserRouter`/`<RouterProvider>`).
